methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Series C → next-stage step-up multiplier (1.7x) to $2.4B last post money.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.rationale: Median across 3 same-sector + same-stage peers in Biotech & Health. Range: $810M - $4.0B.

methods.name: Public-Comp Anchoring
methods.skip_reason: target revenue not on file (private companies rarely disclose)
methods.rationale: Public Biotech & Health P/S median is 6.1x across 3 tickers. Cannot apply without target's revenue.

private.rationale: Same sector (Biotech & Health), same stage (Series C), post-money $810M

data.narrative:
Insitro, a Series C biotech company founded by Daphne Koller, is valued at $2.4B post-money following $1.1B+ in total capital raised. The valuation sits within a peer set of 3 public comparables (RXRX, TEM, ONT.L) and 8 private peers in the Biotech & Health sector at the same stage, including Ultima Genomics ($4.0B), Colossal Biosciences ($1.5B), and New Limit ($810M).

Two valuation methods were applicable. Stage-progression analysis, applying a typical 1.7x Series C step-up multiplier to the $2.4B last post-money valuation, implies $4.1B. Peer-median analysis across the three same-stage, same-sector comparables yields a median of $1.5B, with a range spanning $810M to $4.0B. Public-comp anchoring could not be applied due to absence of revenue data, though the sector median P/S of 6.1x was noted for reference.

Triangulation of the two applicable methods supports a valuation range of $1.3B (low) to $4.7B (high), with a median of $2.8B. The current $2.4B valuation sits at the lower-middle of this range. Key uncertainties include the wide dispersion among peer valuations—a 4.9x spread between the lowest and highest—and the absence of revenue disclosure, which constrains ability to apply public-market multiples. Stage timing and capital-efficiency metrics would refine the range further.
